Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

```xml
<summary>
  <request>User decided to keep three zombie skills (knowhow-accumulation, non-engineer-agent-design, skill-creator) after reviewing cleanup options and fixes for fact recall and uk-conventions false positive</request>
  
  <investigated>Three maintenance items from skilllint: (1) fact hit-log always showing 0 hits despite 195 facts in database, (2) uk-conventions appearing as orphaned skill, (3) three zombie skills (use_count=0, unpinned) in .claude/skills that also exist as junctions synced from AI-canonical repo</investigated>
  
  <learned>Fact recall failure root cause: facts table has 195 entries but embedding_cache join overlap is 0 - embeddings were never computed for facts, only for queries. This caused vectorSearch to return empty results, leaving hybrid scoring with only BM25×0.3 + time decay, almost always below 0.2 threshold. uk-conventions is a custom command in AI-canonical-corp/commands/, not a skill, so missing SKILL.md is expected. The three zombie skills have intact source files in G:\AI\AI-canonical\skills\general\ and are re-junctioned every sync.ps1 run, so removing only junctions would be temporary</learned>
  
  <completed>Implemented backfillFactEmbeddings() in memory-db.ts (idempotent, fills gaps only), added fire-and-forget embedding call to insertFact, added non-blocking historical backfill in index.ts startup. Modified skilllint prompt in memory.ts to skip orphan reporting for entries with "非 skill / false positive" notes (preserves notes on markUsed updates). Code passed npx tsc --noEmit. User selected "keep" option for zombie skills</completed>
  
  <next_steps>Add the three skills back to skill-usage store or mark them as pinned, update STATE.md to mark all three items as resolved with decision rationale, commit bridge repo changes (memory-db.ts, index.ts, memory.ts), build and restart bridge service for fixes to take effect</next_steps>
  
  <notes>The fact embedding fix addresses a fundamental recall issue that has prevented facts from ever being retrieved via vector similarity. uk-conventions fix prevents recurring false positives by preserving manual notes through auto-updates. Keeping the zombie skills maintains access to general-purpose tooling (especially skill-creator) even though current use_count is 0</notes>
</summary>
```

This is creating a memory document that summarizes the three fixes from the dream loop high-priority items. It's documenting:

1. The fact embedding backfill issue - root cause that facts were never getting embeddings computed, causing vector search to fail silently
2. The zombie skill cleanup decision - user chose to keep them, notes about how sync.ps1 rebuilds junctions so deleting junctions alone won't work
3. The uk-conventions false positive - it's a custom command, not a skill, and deleting the entry doesn't work because markUsed auto-rebuilds it

The key insight documented is that all three had "surface-level fixes that don't work" traps - and the lesson is to verify assumptions by checking data flow and actual DB/store state before implementing fixes.

This is a significant documentation artifact capturing learned knowledge about how the system works and common pitfalls.

```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Documented fact embedding gap root cause and three maintenance trap patterns</title>
  <subtitle>Created memory card capturing silent failure mode in hybrid search and lessons from three maintenance issues</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Created fact-embedding-backfill-gap.md memory card documenting root cause analysis and resolution of three dream loop high-priority items</fact>
    <fact>Hybrid search silent failure: vectorSearch joins embedding_cache but only queries get embeddings computed, not facts - 195 facts had 0 join overlap with embedding_cache</fact>
    <fact>Junction deletion trap: sync.ps1 rebuilds all junctions from AI-canonical source, so deleting junctions alone is never a durable skill removal strategy</fact>
    <fact>Store entry deletion trap: markUsed auto-rebuilds entries with orphan=true when skill is used, so deleting entries doesn't persist - must fix in skilllint prompt rules</fact>
    <fact>Common pattern across all three issues: surface-level fixes fail because they don't account for automated rebuild/regeneration mechanisms in the system</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Created a project memory card documenting the investigation and resolution of three maintenance issues flagged by the dream loop. The most critical finding was the fact recall failure: the hybrid search implementation in memory-db.ts joins facts with embedding_cache, but the codebase only calls getEmbeddingCached for queries, never for facts. This resulted in 195 facts having zero overlap with the embedding cache, causing the vector similarity component to always return empty results. The hybrid score could only reach 0.3×time_decay (BM25 weight), almost always below the 0.2 threshold. The key lesson documented is that hybrid search systems with multiple retrieval paths (BM25 + vector) require independent verification that both paths have data - join-based retrieval with an empty result set is a silent failure mode. The memory card also documents two other trap patterns: junction-based skill cleanup fails because sync.ps1 regenerates them, and store entry deletion fails because markUsed auto-rebuilds orphan entries. The overarching pattern is to verify assumptions by checking actual data flow and database/store state before implementing fixes, rather than taking surface-level diagnoses at face value.</narrative>
```
